title: Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary
tagline: Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary, or simply Kalatop Sanctuary, is a 30.69 km2 animal sanctuary at Kalatop and Khajjiar in the Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary, or simply Kalatop Sanctuary, is a 30.69 km2 animal sanctuary at Kalatop and Khajjiar in the Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The sanctuary area is well laid out for trekking trails both at Kalatop and Khajjiar. There is a dense deodar and fir forest covering 19.63 km2 of the sanctuary, which is about 6 km from Dalhousie. Pheasants, serow and black bear are some of the common animals found here.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Dalhousie Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Dalhousie cantonment is a town in Dalhousie, just 50 km from Chamba town in Chamba district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Dalhousie, India
topPlaces.description: Dalhousie (Hindi pronunciation: [ɖəlɦɔːziː]) is a hill station, near the town of Chamba in Chamba district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It is situated across five hills and has an elevation of 1,970 m (6,460 ft) above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Pohlani Mata Temple
topPlaces.description: Jai Pohlani Mata Temple is a Hindu temple for the deity Pohlani Mata. It is located about 8.1 km away from Dalhousie, Himachal Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Khajjiar Lake
topPlaces.description: Khajjiar Lake is situated in Khajjiar, in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated at a height of around 1,951 m above sea level between Dalhousie and Chamba Town.

topPlaces.name: Khajjiar
topPlaces.description: Khajjiar is a hill station near the town of Chamba in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India. Located approximately 24 kilometres (15 mi) from Dalhousie, it lies on a small plateau about 2,000 meters (6,500 feet) above sea level in the foothills of the Dhauladhar ranges in the Western Himalayas.

topPlaces.name: Chamera Dam
topPlaces.description: The Chamera Dam is a concrete gravity dam across the River Ravi, located near the town of Dalhousie, in the Chamba district in the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. A large proportion of the resultant reservoir, called Chamera Lake, lies in the Salooni sub-division of Chamba and was established for the purpose of generating hydroelectricity.

topPlaces.name: Chamrauli, Chamba
topPlaces.description: Chamrauli is a village located in Chamba Tehsil of Chamba district in Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated 19 km away from Chamba, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Chamrauli village.

topPlaces.name: Banikhet
topPlaces.description: Banikhet is located about seven kilometers from Dalhousie, India. It is an important centre of tourism in the Chamba district.

items.note: Dalhousie cantonment is a town in Dalhousie, just 50 km from Chamba town in Chamba district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India.

Dalhousie (Hindi pronunciation: [ɖəlɦɔːziː]) is a hill station, near the town of Chamba in Chamba district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It is situated across five hills and has an elevation of 1,970 m (6,460 ft) above sea level.
Jai Pohlani Mata Temple is a Hindu temple for the deity Pohlani Mata. It is located about 8.1 km away from Dalhousie, Himachal Pradesh, India.
Khajjiar Lake is situated in Khajjiar, in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated at a height of around 1,951 m above sea level between Dalhousie and Chamba Town.

Khajjiar is a hill station near the town of Chamba in Chamba district of Himachal Pradesh, India. Located approximately 24 kilometres (15 mi) from Dalhousie, it lies on a small plateau about 2,000 meters (6,500 feet) above sea level in the foothills of the Dhauladhar ranges in the Western Himalayas.

The Chamera Dam is a concrete gravity dam across the River Ravi, located near the town of Dalhousie, in the Chamba district in the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. A large proportion of the resultant reservoir, called Chamera Lake, lies in the Salooni sub-division of Chamba and was established for the purpose of generating hydroelectricity.

Chamrauli is a village located in Chamba Tehsil of Chamba district in Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated 19 km away from Chamba, which is both district & sub-district headquarter of Chamrauli village.
Banikhet is located about seven kilometers from Dalhousie, India. It is an important centre of tourism in the Chamba district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (49 km) and the nearest railway station is Jammu Tawi (109 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalatop Khajjiar Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dalhousie Cantonment, Dalhousie, India, Pohlani Mata Temple, Khajjiar Lake, Khajjiar.
